items.title: POSCO Factory Agent Project
items.tools: Time-series Vector DB, LLM Agents, Process Signal Representation
items.summary: Building an agent-readable representation layer for large-scale industrial process signals.
highlights: Started a factory-agent project for large-scale process signal understanding.
Building a time-series vector database so agents can retrieve and reason over industrial process signals.
Designing representations that make high-dimensional factory signals accessible to LLM-based agents.

items.title: Agentic Vulnerability Analysis in AI Agents (Project Leader)
items.tools: LLM Agents, Prompt Injection Evaluation, XAI, Multi-Agent Analysis
items.summary: National broadcast collaboration on LLM agent vulnerability analysis.
highlights: Led a 4-person team to analyze real-world vulnerabilities of LLM agents featured in a national broadcast.
Conducted indirect prompt injection evaluation, identifying up to 55% attack success rate in GPT-5.2.
Developed the PnP-XAI app for interpretable LLM behavior analysis and demonstration.
Built and analyzed OpenClaw and Moltbook, revealing failures in multi-agent environments.

items.title: LLM Interpretability Research
items.summary: Mechanistic interpretability of large language models to investigate bias representations.
highlights: Studying bias representation in large language models through neuron- and circuit-level analysis.
Collaborating with researchers at the NYU Global AI Frontier Lab co-directed by Yann LeCun and Kyunghyun Cho.

items.title: Unmanned Swarm CPS Research
items.tools: ROS, Gazebo, Webotsg, Python, UAV/UGV Simulation
items.summary: Multi-agent reinforcement learning and sim-to-real systems for unmanned swarms.
highlights: Developed multi-agent reinforcement learning algorithms for patrol and communication tasks in unmanned swarms.
Implemented sim-to-real transfer using domain adaptation to bridge physical and simulated environments.
Built ROS-based communication pipelines between Gazebo and Webots for UAVs and UGVs.
Produced multiple papers and patents through the project pipeline.

items.title: AI for Cosmetic Composition (Phase 2)
items.summary: A web-based AI service for cosmetic composition design and property inference.
highlights: Advanced AlchemyNet with domain knowledge for cosmetic property prediction.
Built a deployable interface for composition design and physicochemical inference.
Submitted one journal paper and one conference paper under review.

items.title: AI for Cosmetic Composition (Phase 1)
items.tools: Transformer, Python, Scikit-learn, Pandas, Visualization Libraries
items.summary: Neural modeling of cosmetic formulations and explainable latent spaces.
highlights: Developed AlchemyNet, a neural model inspired by alchemical formulation structure.
Predicted viscosity, pH, density, hardness, and related physicochemical properties.
Designed explainable embedding spaces connecting chemical composition with sensory attributes.

items.title: X-Ray Object Detection and Saliency
items.summary: Attribution-based analysis of overlapping object detection decisions.
highlights: Applied attribution-based XAI methods to improve interpretability of X-ray object detection models.
Analyzed decision-making in overlapping object cases to improve model transparency.
